page: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 8 MB L3 caches, alongside support for AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or a GPU, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test and Geekbench single-core score placing in the top 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 10%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling in the middle 50%. Tradeoffs are visible in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang, which rank in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes, along with Redis SET operations and static web serving, perform within average ranges.

From a resource density perspective, the r8a.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high memory capacity without the expense of additional compute cores. It is suited for memory-intensive applications such as database operations, caching layers like Redis, text processing, and lightweight web serving. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation, or large-scale LLM inference, where its prompt processing speeds for medium models fall into the bottom 10%.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance, though multi-threaded tasks and software compilation fall into average or lower tiers. Qualitatively, its 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio offers cost-efficient resource allocation for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching, and text processing rather than heavy multi-threaded compute or large-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, optimized for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.large features 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for database operations, caching, and text processing
